symbols: Christ bound to pillar — the Lamb of God submitting to punishment
whips and scourges — the instruments of suffering prophesied by Isaiah
soldiers mocking — the world's hatred against the righteous one
description: A painting by Jerg Ratgeb depicting the Flagellation of Christ. Jesus is bound to a pillar while Roman soldiers strike him with whips and scourges. The scene is violent and intense, rendered in late medieval German style with vivid detail and emotional intensity.
theological_description: The Flagellation (JHN.19.1; MAT.27.26) is one of the most brutal episodes of the Passion. Pilate had Jesus flogged, hoping to satisfy the crowd without crucifixion. The scourging fulfills Isaiah's Suffering Servant prophecy: 'By his wounds we are healed' (ISA.53.5). Peter later testified: 'He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. By his wounds you have been healed' (1PE.2.24). Christ's silence before his tormentors fulfills Isaiah: 'Like a sheep before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth' (ISA.53.7).

scripture_refs.reason: By his wounds we are healed
He bore our sins, by his wounds you are healed
Like a sheep before shearers, he opened not his mouth
